description: Scripted-truth capture-corpus session catalogue. Doctrine in docs/scripted-truth-corpus.md. status: active | retired (retired entries stay listed with a reason — removals are loud, never silent).

sessions.notes: clicks on button/image/plain elements; carries title, alt_text and structural-css locator coverage
type then blur by clicking a neutral button (never Tab — correlation race); name + placeholder coverage
keyboard-driven native select (click + ArrowDown x2 + Enter); selectOption is untrusted and never used
captured control keys on a focused input (Enter, Escape)
link navigation, typed navigation, reload — all loopback

user click on a target=_blank link: the click is the action, the tab is its effect — truth records the click + the new context chrome-initiated navigate. The missing context_open is #252 (a sufficiency-lint fail, not a corpus diff); context_switch/close have no trusted-input driver — loudly excluded in docs/scripted-truth-corpus.md
clicks in the top document and inside an iframe; frame_src null vs frame URL
file chooser with buffer-supplied file (deterministic name/size/mime)
native HTML5 drag and drop: drag_start + drop with source_element
container scroll past the significance floor; one settled scroll action. Current capture records nothing: the burst baseline is anchored post-first-movement (#251)
clicks whose handlers trigger programmatic scroll/focus/value changes; truth is the clicks only (action-vs-effect boundary; docent#224 class boundary)
password + cc-number typing (masked values, redacted flags) and a sensitive select with visible text (masked-in-place text locator entry)
type then click submit within the suppression window; truth carries the type action current capture loses
setPointerCapture slider drag; truth carries drag_start+drop current capture loses
sub-floor container scroll; truth carries the scroll current capture discards
net-zero jiggle inside one debounce window; truth carries the settled net-zero scroll (#232). Produced delta reflects the post-movement baseline anchor (#251)
activation context_switch (the first click activates the never-raised window — deterministic via the primer window) + one deliberate click
activation context_switch + two rapid clicks; no double_click type exists in the format, so truth is two click actions
user clicks session window then primer window: two activation context_switches + clicks; the primer teardown reverts foreground to the session window (trailing click-correlated context_switch)

input-correlated application selection: activation context_switch + click, then a same-root EVENT_OBJECT_SELECTION fired past the click-redundancy window is captured as select (negatives pinned in capture_integration.rs completeness module)

wheel scroll over a really-scrolling multiline EDIT: truth names the element and the nonzero position; current capture fabricates scroll_top/scroll_left 0.0 with element null (#228) — the two baseline entries are exactly that defect pair
one wheel notch over the really-scrolling EDIT — under the significance floor, current capture discards the scroll entirely; truth carries it (missing-action baseline entry)

click-to-focus the empty EDIT, then per-char layout-VK typing at human cadence: printables buffer and the value-change coalesces one type(hello). AUTHORING NOTES: enigo.text() injects VK_PACKET keystrokes that leak as phantom key(VK_0xE7) actions nondeterministically (unicode-injection/IME class — issue candidate); under post-compile load, partial key leaks (key(E),key(L)) were observed once — supersede race, watch on CI. element.text is stably the first value-change snapshot (h)

the d-type-edit pattern against an ES_PASSWORD EDIT: native IsPassword masking at capture — truth pins the exact mask, redacted flag, nulled text, and untouched identity-derived locators (the desktop chokepoint contract, on a real capture)

click on a plain custom-class window (DefWindowProc, no accessibility content) — deterministic coordinate-mode fallback: Window element, no locators, window_rect present. WATCH: selector embeds the virtual-desktop name (Desktop 1) and mode-vs-docs drift (selector is a tree path, docs say coord:x,y; tag Window, docs say unknown) — CI adjudicates the former; the latter joins the window_rect doc-drift note
